description:
You are a web-developer who has just received a raw tracking export in CSV format.  
Your job is to assemble a very small reporting pipeline (no more than 3-4 terminal commands are needed) that:

1. Reads the file  
   /home/user/projects/webapp/data/visits.csv  

2. Filters the rows that simultaneously satisfy BOTH of the following criteria  
   • The value in the “browser” column is exactly “Chrome” (case-sensitive)  
   • The value in the “status” column is exactly 200  

3. Counts how many rows remain after that filtering step.

4. Creates a new JSON file named  
   /home/user/projects/webapp/reports/chrome_success_summary.json  

   The file must be overwritten if it already exists and must contain **exactly** the following two-field JSON object (strict whitespace and key order matter, so reproduce the example verbatim):  

   {
     "report_generated": "2024-05-04",
     "total_successful_chrome_visits": N
   }

   where N is the integer count obtained in step 3.  There must be:
   • one space after every colon,  
   • no trailing spaces on any line,  
   • a newline character at the very end of the file.

5. Generates a tiny plaintext log called  
   /home/user/projects/webapp/reports/generate_report.log  

   The log must consist of **exactly two lines** with a final newline after the second line:

   Extraction complete  
   Report saved to chrome_success_summary.json

When you are finished, only the two new files (the JSON report and the log file) should have been added or modified inside /home/user/projects/webapp/reports.  No other directories or files should be touched.

Verify your work by showing the contents of both newly created files in the terminal.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the task begins:

1. Directory /home/user/projects/webapp/ exists and is owned by user.
2. Sub-directory /home/user/projects/webapp/data/ exists.
3. Sub-directory /home/user/projects/webapp/reports/ exists and may already contain other unrelated files; the agent is allowed to overwrite ONLY the two filenames mentioned in the public task.
4. File /home/user/projects/webapp/data/visits.csv already exists with the exact contents (including final newline):

date,user_id,browser,status
2024-05-01,101,Chrome,200
2024-05-01,102,Firefox,200
2024-05-01,103,Chrome,404
2024-05-02,104,Chrome,200
2024-05-02,105,Edge,200
2024-05-03,106,Chrome,200

Expected state AFTER successful task completion:

A. File /home/user/projects/webapp/reports/chrome_success_summary.json must exist with exact content (final newline included):  
{
  "report_generated": "2024-05-04",
  "total_successful_chrome_visits": 3
}

B. File /home/user/projects/webapp/reports/generate_report.log must exist with exact content (final newline included):  
Extraction complete
Report saved to chrome_success_summary.json

Both files must be world-readable (permission mode 644 or more permissive).  No other files are to be added or altered inside /home/user/projects/webapp/.  The CSV file must remain unchanged.
